question: Does the model conclude that Option B is correct (Work done against friction is 5J)?
Does the model conclude that Option C is correct (The distance between point A and point B is √(7/12) m)?
Does the model explicitly identify that Option A is incorrect (coefficient of kinetic friction cannot remain constant)?
Does the model explicitly identify that Option D is incorrect (force exerted by the ball on the track is not constant)?
Does the model correctly calculate the vertical displacement from A to B as y = R·cos(60°) = 0.5 m?
Does the model correctly use the condition that velocity at B is tangent to the arc to relate the horizontal and vertical velocity components (specifically, vy/vx = tan(60°) = √3)?
Does the model correctly use kinematic equations (e.g., y = ½gt²) to calculate the time of flight or vertical velocity at point B?
Does the model correctly calculate the horizontal displacement x (e.g., 1/√3 m) and combine it with the vertical displacement to find the distance AB?
Does the model correctly identify that 'uniform circular motion' implies constant kinetic energy along the arc BC?
Does the model apply the Work-Energy Theorem or Conservation of Energy to conclude that the work done against friction equals the work done by gravity during the motion from B to C?
Does the model correctly calculate the vertical height difference between B and C as h = R(1 - cos(60°)) = 0.5 m?
Does the model correctly explain that the normal force varies because the radial component of gravity (mg·cosθ) changes with position?
Does the model correctly reason that the coefficient of friction cannot be constant because the friction force (balancing tangential gravity) and normal force vary differently with position?
Does the model correctly use the given numerical values (R=1m, m=1kg, g=10m/s², ∠BOC=60°) consistently throughout the calculations?
Does the generated diagram correctly depict point A at the same horizontal level (height) as the center O?
Is point C depicted directly below the center O, and is the arc BC shown as the correct sector corresponding to the 60° angle?
Does the trajectory from A to B appear as a parabolic curve (projectile motion) rather than a straight line?
If velocity vectors are drawn at point B, is the resultant velocity shown tangent to the arc (perpendicular to radius OB)?
If a free-body diagram is generated, is gravity shown pointing vertically downwards and the normal force perpendicular to the surface (radial) regardless of position?
Is the central angle ∠BOC (or the angle of radius OB) clearly marked or visually consistent with 60°?
Does the visual depiction of the angle ∠BOC align with the text description (i.e., the image actually shows an angle of approximately 60°)?
If the text describes the velocity at B as tangent, does the corresponding diagram show the velocity vector perpendicular to the radius OB?
If the text calculates specific displacements (e.g., vertical drop is half the radius), does the diagram visually reflect these relative proportions?
